name: remotion-render
description: Render videos from React/Remotion component code via inference.sh. Pass TSX code, get MP4. Supports all Remotion APIs: useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, interpolate, AbsoluteFill, Sequence. Configurable resolution, FPS, duration, codec. Use for: programmatic video generation, animated graphics, motion design, data-driven videos, React animations to video. Triggers: remotion, render video from code, tsx to video, react video, programmatic video, remotion render, code to video, animated video, motion graphics code, react animation video
capabilities: Execute Remotion video renders via the inference.sh platform CLI
Support all Remotion APIs including useCurrentFrame, useVideoConfig, spring, and interpolate
Configure video output parameters such as resolution, frame rate, duration, and codec
Pass dynamic props to Remotion components for data-driven video content
Stream rendering progress updates during video generation
Return downloadable MP4 video URLs upon render completion
author: inference-sh-9
securitySummary: Static analysis detected 31 external command patterns and 8 network URLs, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The skill uses the infsh CLI with proper allowed-tools constraints (Bash(infsh *)). External commands are intentional platform integration, not arbitrary execution. Network URLs point to legitimate service endpoints. Weak crypto finding triggered by SHA-256 documentation reference (SHA-256 is secure). Pipe-to-shell on line 17 is optional user-run CLI installation, not skill-executed code.
valueStatement: Creating videos programmatically is complex and requires specialized rendering infrastructure. This skill transforms React Remotion components into MP4 videos through cloud-based rendering, enabling developers to generate videos from code without managing rendering servers.
userTitle: Render Videos from React Remotion Code
limitations: Requires active internet connection to access inference.sh cloud rendering servers
Only supports Remotion-compatible React component code structures
Video duration and resolution constrained by inference.sh platform limits
Cannot perform local video rendering without the inference.sh service

useCases.title: Automated Video Content Generation
useCases.description: Marketing teams can generate personalized video content at scale by passing customer data as props to Remotion templates, producing customized videos for each recipient without manual editing.

useCases.title: Data Visualization Videos
useCases.description: Developers can convert dynamic charts, graphs, and data animations into shareable video formats for presentations, social media, or documentation purposes.

useCases.title: Animated UI Prototypes
useCases.description: Design teams can export interactive React animations as video files for stakeholder reviews, design documentation, or handoff materials without screen recording.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Fade Animation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a simple Remotion component that fades in a welcome message over 3 seconds at 1080p resolution. Use the remotion-render skill to generate the video.
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner learning basic Remotion animation concepts

promptTemplates.title: Animated Counter with Progress
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a video showing a percentage counter animating from 0 to 100 over 5 seconds. Include a loading indicator below the counter. Use Remotion's useCurrentFrame and useVideoConfig hooks.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating loading state animations for applications

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Scene Sequence Animation
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a Remotion component with three sequential title cards that fade in one after another. Each card should display for 2 seconds before transitioning. Use Sequence components and spring animations for smooth motion.
promptTemplates.scenario: Producing intro sequences for video content

promptTemplates.title: Data-Driven Personalized Video
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a Remotion template that accepts user data props including name, company, and achievement. Generate a personalized award certificate animation. Then render multiple videos by passing different prop values for each recipient.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generating customized videos at scale for events or campaigns

bestPractices: Keep Remotion components pure and side-effect free for reliable rendering across different environments
Use Remotion's interpolate and spring functions for smooth, professional animations instead of manual frame calculations
Test components locally with Remotion preview before submitting to cloud rendering to catch errors early
antiPatterns: Avoid making API calls or network requests inside Remotion components since rendering happens in an isolated cloud environment
Do not use browser-only APIs like localStorage or window object as Remotion renders in a Node.js context
Avoid excessively long video durations or extremely high resolutions that may timeout or exceed platform limits

faq.question: What is Remotion and how does this skill work?
faq.answer: Remotion is a React framework for creating videos using code. This skill sends your Remotion TSX components to the inference.sh cloud platform, which renders them as MP4 videos and returns a downloadable URL.

faq.question: Do I need to install anything to use this skill?
faq.answer: The skill uses the inference.sh CLI internally. You need an inference.sh account with valid credentials configured. The CLI can be installed via curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh

faq.question: What video formats and codecs are supported?
faq.answer: The platform outputs MP4 files with H.264 codec by default. Resolution is configurable up to platform limits. Common presets include 1920x1080, 1080x1080, and 1280x720.

faq.question: Can I pass dynamic data to my video components?
faq.answer: Yes, use the props parameter to pass any JSON-serializable data to your component. Your component receives these as React props for data-driven video generation.

faq.question: How long can my videos be?
faq.answer: Video duration depends on your inference.sh plan. Typical limits range from 30 seconds for free tiers to several minutes for paid plans. Check your account settings for specific limits.

faq.question: What happens if my render fails?
faq.answer: The skill returns an error message with details about the failure. Common issues include syntax errors in TSX code, missing imports, or exceeding resource limits. Fix the error and resubmit.
